title: NimbusWorks platform overview
text:
NimbusWorks runs a microservice fleet operated via the `nbx` CLI. There are
10 production services across 4 teams. Each service has a
health status (healthy/degraded/down), an error rate, a replica count, and a
per-service connection pool. Teams and on-call ownership:
- Core Platform: lead Farida Rahman, on-call #oncall-coreplat
- Data Services: lead Tomasz Krol, on-call #oncall-data
- Edge & Traffic: lead Ines Delgado, on-call #oncall-edge
- Payments: lead Kwame Mensah, on-call #oncall-payments
Exactly one service is ever the ROOT CAUSE of an incident; every other alerting
service is a cascading symptom of an unhealthy dependency.

title: Service handbook: gatekeeper
text:
Service `gatekeeper` is owned by the Edge & Traffic team. API gateway; terminates TLS and routes external requests. It listens on port 7443
and has an SLO of 120 ms p99. It depends on: tokensmith. Services that depend on
gatekeeper (its dependents): none (leaf consumer). Canonical config key `upstream_timeout_ms` with healthy value 2000. This service does not front a sized connection pool.
When gatekeeper is the root cause, its dependents show upstream connect timeouts
(NBX-3301) even though they are individually healthy; restarting a
dependent does nothing until gatekeeper itself is fixed.

title: Service handbook: tokensmith
text:
Service `tokensmith` is owned by the Core Platform team. issues and validates NimbusAuth session tokens. It listens on port 7020
and has an SLO of 40 ms p99. It depends on: vaultkeep. Services that depend on
tokensmith (its dependents): gatekeeper, ledgerline. Canonical config key `validation_timeout_ms` with healthy value 3000. This service maintains a database connection pool; the healthy pool_max is >= 512.
When tokensmith is the root cause, its dependents show upstream connect timeouts
(NBX-3301) even though they are individually healthy; restarting a
dependent does nothing until tokensmith itself is fixed.

title: Service handbook: vaultkeep
text:
Service `vaultkeep` is owned by the Core Platform team. secrets and key-material store. It listens on port 7030
and has an SLO of 25 ms p99. It depends on: none (leaf dependency). Services that depend on
vaultkeep (its dependents): tokensmith. Canonical config key `cache_ttl_s` with healthy value 300. This service does not front a sized connection pool.
When vaultkeep is the root cause, its dependents show upstream connect timeouts
(NBX-3301) even though they are individually healthy; restarting a
dependent does nothing until vaultkeep itself is fixed.

title: Service handbook: ledgerline
text:
Service `ledgerline` is owned by the Payments team. payment ledger; records all transactions. It listens on port 7210
and has an SLO of 200 ms p99. It depends on: tokensmith, quillbase. Services that depend on
ledgerline (its dependents): courierbot. Canonical config key `batch_size` with healthy value 100. This service maintains a database connection pool; the healthy pool_max is >= 512.
When ledgerline is the root cause, its dependents show upstream connect timeouts
(NBX-3301) even though they are individually healthy; restarting a
dependent does nothing until ledgerline itself is fixed.

title: Service handbook: quillbase
text:
Service `quillbase` is owned by the Data Services team. primary OLTP database proxy. It listens on port 7305
and has an SLO of 60 ms p99. It depends on: none (leaf dependency). Services that depend on
quillbase (its dependents): glacierdocs, ledgerline, streamforge. Canonical config key `statement_timeout_ms` with healthy value 5000. This service maintains a database connection pool; the healthy pool_max is >= 512.
When quillbase is the root cause, its dependents show upstream connect timeouts
(NBX-3301) even though they are individually healthy; restarting a
dependent does nothing until quillbase itself is fixed.

title: Service handbook: streamforge
text:
Service `streamforge` is owned by the Data Services team. event streaming and change-data-capture pipeline. It listens on port 7320
and has an SLO of 90 ms p99. It depends on: quillbase. Services that depend on
streamforge (its dependents): courierbot, pulseboard. Canonical config key `consumer_max_poll` with healthy value 500. This service does not front a sized connection pool.
When streamforge is the root cause, its dependents show upstream connect timeouts
(NBX-3301) even though they are individually healthy; restarting a
dependent does nothing until streamforge itself is fixed.

title: Service handbook: glacierdocs
text:
Service `glacierdocs` is owned by the Data Services team. document archive and retrieval service. It listens on port 7340
and has an SLO of 300 ms p99. It depends on: quillbase. Services that depend on
glacierdocs (its dependents): none (leaf consumer). Canonical config key `fetch_timeout_ms` with healthy value 4000. This service maintains a database connection pool; the healthy pool_max is >= 512.
When glacierdocs is the root cause, its dependents show upstream connect timeouts
(NBX-3301) even though they are individually healthy; restarting a
dependent does nothing until glacierdocs itself is fixed.

title: Service handbook: pulseboard
text:
Service `pulseboard` is owned by the Core Platform team. internal metrics and alerting dashboard. It listens on port 7050
and has an SLO of 150 ms p99. It depends on: streamforge. Services that depend on
pulseboard (its dependents): none (leaf consumer). Canonical config key `refresh_interval_s` with healthy value 30. This service does not front a sized connection pool.
When pulseboard is the root cause, its dependents show upstream connect timeouts
(NBX-3301) even though they are individually healthy; restarting a
dependent does nothing until pulseboard itself is fixed.

title: Service handbook: mistral-cache
text:
Service `mistral-cache` is owned by the Edge & Traffic team. edge response cache (LRU, 64GB per node). It listens on port 7460
and has an SLO of 15 ms p99. It depends on: none (leaf dependency). Services that depend on
mistral-cache (its dependents): none (leaf consumer). Canonical config key `region_prefix_required` with healthy value true. This service does not front a sized connection pool.
When mistral-cache is the root cause, its dependents show upstream connect timeouts
(NBX-3301) even though they are individually healthy; restarting a
dependent does nothing until mistral-cache itself is fixed.

title: Service handbook: courierbot
text:
Service `courierbot` is owned by the Payments team. sends receipts and payment notifications. It listens on port 7230
and has an SLO of 250 ms p99. It depends on: ledgerline, streamforge. Services that depend on
courierbot (its dependents): none (leaf consumer). Canonical config key `retry_backoff_ms` with healthy value 250. This service does not front a sized connection pool.
When courierbot is the root cause, its dependents show upstream connect timeouts
(NBX-3301) even though they are individually healthy; restarting a
dependent does nothing until courierbot itself is fixed.

title: Runbook NBX-1101
text:
Error NBX-1101 on `tokensmith`: token signing key rotation failed. Remediation: run `nbx keys rotate --service tokensmith --force`, then restart tokensmith. Confirm recovery with
`nbx status tokensmith` showing status healthy and error rate below 1%.

title: Runbook NBX-1102
text:
Error NBX-1102 on `tokensmith`: token validation latency above SLO. Remediation: check vaultkeep health first; if healthy, scale tokensmith to 4 replicas. Confirm recovery with
`nbx status tokensmith` showing status healthy and error rate below 1%.

title: Runbook NBX-2201
text:
Error NBX-2201 on `quillbase`: connection pool exhausted. Remediation: raise pool_max to 512 via `nbx config set quillbase pool_max 512` and restart. Confirm recovery with
`nbx status quillbase` showing status healthy and error rate below 1%.

title: Runbook NBX-2202
text:
Error NBX-2202 on `quillbase`: replication lag above 30s. Remediation: pause streamforge consumers, let lag drain below 5s, then resume. Confirm recovery with
`nbx status quillbase` showing status healthy and error rate below 1%.

title: Runbook NBX-3301
text:
Error NBX-3301 on `gatekeeper`: upstream connect timeout. Remediation: check tokensmith health; gatekeeper retries do NOT apply to POST routes. Confirm recovery with
`nbx status gatekeeper` showing status healthy and error rate below 1%.

title: Runbook NBX-3302
text:
Error NBX-3302 on `mistral-cache`: cache hit rate below 40%. Remediation: verify cache keys include the region prefix; flush with `nbx cache flush --node all`. Confirm recovery with
`nbx status mistral-cache` showing status healthy and error rate below 1%.

title: Runbook NBX-4401
text:
Error NBX-4401 on `ledgerline`: double-entry imbalance detected. Remediation: freeze writes with `nbx ledger freeze`, run `nbx ledger reconcile`, page Payments lead. Confirm recovery with
`nbx status ledgerline` showing status healthy and error rate below 1%.

title: Runbook NBX-5501
text:
Error NBX-5501 on `streamforge`: consumer group stalled. Remediation: run `nbx stream rewind --group <group> --to-checkpoint` then unpause. Confirm recovery with
`nbx status streamforge` showing status healthy and error rate below 1%.

title: Fault playbook: bad_config
text:
Incident class `bad_config`. Error code NBX-6600. A configuration value drifted out of its valid range. A bare restart re-loads the SAME bad value and does NOT fix it. Correct sequence: `set_config` the canonical key to its healthy value, THEN `restart` so the new value takes effect.
Always identify the single root-cause service first (the one whose logs show a
real fault code, not the cascade code NBX-3301), fix it, then verify with
check_all that every service returned to healthy.

title: Fault playbook: bad_deploy
text:
Incident class `bad_deploy`. Error code NBX-6700. Error rate spiked immediately after the most recent deploy. A restart just re-launches the same bad release. Correct fix: `rollback` the service to the previous release.
Always identify the single root-cause service first (the one whose logs show a
real fault code, not the cascade code NBX-3301), fix it, then verify with
check_all that every service returned to healthy.

title: Fault playbook: pool_exhausted
text:
Incident class `pool_exhausted`. Error code NBX-2201. The connection pool is saturated. A restart alone keeps the tiny pool. Correct sequence: `set_config` pool_max to >= 512, THEN `restart`.
Always identify the single root-cause service first (the one whose logs show a
real fault code, not the cascade code NBX-3301), fix it, then verify with
check_all that every service returned to healthy.

title: Fault playbook: crash
text:
Incident class `crash`. Error code NBX-6900. The process stopped answering health checks. This is the ONLY class a plain `restart` of the ROOT service fixes. Restarting a cascaded symptom instead of the root does nothing.
Always identify the single root-cause service first (the one whose logs show a
real fault code, not the cascade code NBX-3301), fix it, then verify with
check_all that every service returned to healthy.

title: Dependency fact: gatekeeper -> tokensmith
text:
`gatekeeper` depends on `tokensmith`. If `tokensmith` is unhealthy, `gatekeeper` will report
NBX-3301 upstream connect timeouts and appear down even though `gatekeeper`
itself has no root-cause fault. Fixing `tokensmith` restores `gatekeeper` automatically.

title: Dependency fact: tokensmith -> vaultkeep
text:
`tokensmith` depends on `vaultkeep`. If `vaultkeep` is unhealthy, `tokensmith` will report
NBX-3301 upstream connect timeouts and appear down even though `tokensmith`
itself has no root-cause fault. Fixing `vaultkeep` restores `tokensmith` automatically.

title: Dependency fact: courierbot -> streamforge
text:

title: Policy: deploy-freeze
text: Production deploys are frozen every Friday 18:00 UTC through Monday 06:00 UTC.

title: Policy: sev1
text: A Sev1 incident requires paging the service team lead within 10 minutes and posting to #incident-bridge.

title: Policy: rollback
text: Any deploy showing error-rate increase above 2% within 15 minutes must be rolled back with `nbx deploy rollback <service>`.

title: Policy: secrets
text: Secrets may only be read through vaultkeep; direct file access is a fireable offense.

title: Policy: capacity
text: Services must run at least 2 replicas in prod; payments services at least 3.

title: CLI: nbx status <service>
text: `nbx status <service>` -- show health, replica count, error rate, p99 latency of a service.

title: CLI: nbx logs <service> --tail N
text: `nbx logs <service> --tail N` -- show last N log lines of a service.

title: CLI: nbx deploy rollback <service>
text: `nbx deploy rollback <service>` -- roll a service back to the previous release.

title: CLI: nbx scale <service> --replicas N
text: `nbx scale <service> --replicas N` -- set replica count.

title: CLI: nbx config set <service> <key> <value>
text: `nbx config set <service> <key> <value>` -- set a config value (requires restart).

title: CLI: nbx restart <service>
text: `nbx restart <service>` -- rolling restart of a service.

title: CLI: nbx keys rotate --service <service> --force
text: `nbx keys rotate --service <service> --force` -- force key-material rotation.

title: CLI: nbx ledger freeze
text: `nbx ledger freeze` -- freeze ledgerline writes (Sev1 payments action).

title: CLI: nbx cache flush --node all
text: `nbx cache flush --node all` -- flush mistral-cache on every node.

title: Incident triage methodology
text:
Triage at NimbusWorks: (1) run check_all to see which services are unhealthy.
(2) Distinguish root from symptom: pull get_logs on the deepest-looking broken
service; a NBX-3301 means it is only a symptom -- follow its dependency
upward. (3) The root's logs carry a real fault code
(NBX-6600, NBX-6700, NBX-2201, NBX-6900). (4) Apply the fault-type fix.
(5) Re-run check_all to verify full recovery within the tool-call budget.

title: Why restart-everything fails
text:
Blindly restarting every service is an anti-pattern at NimbusWorks. Only a `crash`
(NBX-6900) is cleared by a restart. bad_config
(NBX-6600), bad_deploy (NBX-6700) and
pool_exhausted (NBX-2201) all survive a restart and need a
set_config+restart, a rollback, or a pool bump + restart respectively.

title: Remediation card: bad_config on gatekeeper
text:
When `gatekeeper` (Edge & Traffic) suffers a bad_config fault it emits NBX-6600. Its
transitive dependents (no downstream services) go down with NBX-3301 as pure symptoms.
Correct remediation (do NOT restart the symptoms): 1) set_config gatekeeper upstream_timeout_ms=2000; 2) restart gatekeeper.
Verify with check_all that all 10 services report healthy.

title: Postmortem: bad_config at gatekeeper
text:
Incident summary. Root cause: a bad_config fault on `gatekeeper`, owned by Edge & Traffic. The
first symptom the on-call saw was multiple alerting services; check_all showed
gatekeeper plus its dependents (no downstream services) unhealthy. get_logs on the dependents
returned NBX-3301 (upstream connect timeout) -- a symptom, not the cause.
get_logs on `gatekeeper` returned NBX-6600: config value 'upstream_timeout_ms' out of valid range; correct it and restart.
Resolution followed the bad_config playbook: 1) set_config gatekeeper upstream_timeout_ms=2000; 2) restart gatekeeper. After the fix, check_all
confirmed all services healthy. Lesson: never restart a NBX-3301 symptom;
walk the dependency chain to the one service with a real fault code.

title: Remediation card: bad_config on tokensmith
text:
When `tokensmith` (Core Platform) suffers a bad_config fault it emits NBX-6600. Its
transitive dependents (courierbot, gatekeeper, ledgerline) go down with NBX-3301 as pure symptoms.
Correct remediation (do NOT restart the symptoms): 1) set_config tokensmith validation_timeout_ms=3000; 2) restart tokensmith.
Verify with check_all that all 10 services report healthy.

title: Postmortem: bad_config at tokensmith
text:

title: Remediation card: bad_config on quillbase

Correct remediation (do NOT restart the symptoms): 1) set_config quillbase statement_timeout_ms=5000; 2) restart quillbase.
Verify with check_all that all 10 services report healthy.

title: Remediation card: bad_deploy on gatekeeper
text:
When `gatekeeper` (Edge & Traffic) suffers a bad_deploy fault it emits NBX-6700. Its
transitive dependents (no downstream services) go down with NBX-3301 as pure symptoms.
Correct remediation (do NOT restart the symptoms): 1) rollback gatekeeper.
Verify with check_all that all 10 services report healthy.

title: Postmortem: bad_deploy at gatekeeper
text:
Incident summary. Root cause: a bad_deploy fault on `gatekeeper`, owned by Edge & Traffic. The
first symptom the on-call saw was multiple alerting services; check_all showed
gatekeeper plus its dependents (no downstream services) unhealthy. get_logs on the dependents
returned NBX-3301 (upstream connect timeout) -- a symptom, not the cause.
get_logs on `gatekeeper` returned NBX-6700: error-rate spiked immediately after the last deploy; roll back the release.
Resolution followed the bad_deploy playbook: 1) rollback gatekeeper. After the fix, check_all
confirmed all services healthy. Lesson: never restart a NBX-3301 symptom;
walk the dependency chain to the one service with a real fault code.

title: Remediation card: pool_exhausted on quillbase
text:
When `quillbase` (Data Services) suffers a pool_exhausted fault it emits NBX-2201. Its
transitive dependents (courierbot, glacierdocs, ledgerline, pulseboard, streamforge) go down with NBX-3301 as pure symptoms.
Correct remediation (do NOT restart the symptoms): 1) set_config quillbase pool_max=512; 2) restart quillbase.
Verify with check_all that all 10 services report healthy.

title: Postmortem: pool_exhausted at quillbase
text:
Incident summary. Root cause: a pool_exhausted fault on `quillbase`, owned by Data Services. The
first symptom the on-call saw was multiple alerting services; check_all showed
quillbase plus its dependents (courierbot, glacierdocs, ledgerline, pulseboard, streamforge) unhealthy. get_logs on the dependents
returned NBX-3301 (upstream connect timeout) -- a symptom, not the cause.
get_logs on `quillbase` returned NBX-2201: connection pool exhausted; raise pool_max to >=512 and restart.
Resolution followed the pool_exhausted playbook: 1) set_config quillbase pool_max=512; 2) restart quillbase. After the fix, check_all
confirmed all services healthy. Lesson: never restart a NBX-3301 symptom;
walk the dependency chain to the one service with a real fault code.
